title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
summary: PostHog is building an automated pipeline that converts product signals like error spikes and user feedback into pull requests via a background agent. The agent groups related errors and session replays, analyzes the codebase, and opens PRs for developers to review. The system collapses the manual triage and ticket workflow into autonomous execution.
writeup: Joshua Snyder of PostHog details an automated agent pipeline that turns product signals into pull requests. The system detects signals like rage clicks, error spikes, and customer messages, groups related issues using embeddings, researches the codebase, and opens PRs. A key design choice: rather than embedding raw signals directly, the pipeline uses LLM-generated queries to improve semantic accuracy when clustering, alongside error recovery and session replay as supporting components.
